title: Top-level src dry-run now reaches ManalinkEh.dll plan; real build remains blocked
evidence: src/card_id.h is restored as an exact copy of Program/src/card_id.h; (cd src && make -n) prints the full compile/link plan; tools/check-build-prereqs.sh --report-only now reports missing yasm, dlltool, objcopy, and windres.
why it matters: Top-level source tree can now express its ManalinkEh.dll build plan, but still cannot prove a real rebuild of the patched runtime DLL on this machine.
suggested fix: Install/document controlled Windows DLL toolchain and keep outputs isolated before any real build.
test strategy: dry-run make; preflight; compare produced DLL hashes only in a throwaway output folder after toolchain setup.

title: Windows-oriented build tools are unavailable locally
evidence: yasm, dlltool, objcopy, and windres are absent; makefiles call them.
why it matters: DLL/resource builds cannot complete on this machine without a toolchain.
suggested fix: Document exact MinGW/yasm/binutils prerequisites and keep build outputs outside runtime folders.
test strategy: Re-run tool availability and make dry-runs after installing tools in a controlled environment.

title: src and Program/src divergence is measured and partly guarded
evidence: Parity report records 80 matching paths, 142 differing paths, and 100 paths missing from Program/src; exact-match files and current safety markers pass.
why it matters: It is still unclear which source snapshot corresponds to shipped DLLs, so source-only fixes may not affect runtime.
suggested fix: Keep the parity guard for mirrored safety edits, then choose/record an authoritative source snapshot before any source-to-binary claim.
test strategy: Run the parity checker; compare build outputs from both trees in throwaway directories if build blockers are resolved.

title: Build helper mutates source when intentionally used, but external copies are now opt-in
evidence: src/build.pl now supports --dry-run, --out-dir, --copy-to, and --legacy-copy-targets; no default c:\magic2k copy remains.
why it matters: Running it casually no longer silently copies to external install paths, though a card-name invocation can still edit ManalinkEh.asm.
suggested fix: Use --dry-run first; add deeper build-output isolation later if needed.
test strategy: Run perl syntax check, help, and dry-run mode from src.

title: Deploy batch destructive path now requires explicit confirmation
evidence: src/deploy.bat now exits before mutation unless called with --confirmed-c-magic2k-deploy; confirmed mode still deletes/copies under c:\magic2k.
why it matters: It is less likely to mutate a Windows packaging tree by accident, but remains historical and hardcoded when explicitly confirmed.
suggested fix: Keep historical; run confirmed mode only in a prepared Windows packaging copy.
test strategy: Static review of the confirmation guard; no Windows packaging run.

title: CardArtLib Cards.dat loader now fails closed on malformed input
evidence: loadDat() now checks fopen results, header reads, data reads, allocation, card ids, name offsets, string termination, and missing names before art lookup.
why it matters: Bad or missing Cards.dat is less likely to crash the process before useful fallback behavior, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a malformed Cards.dat harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static inspection; CardArtLib make dry-runs; malformed Cards.dat copy after build support exists.

title: CardArtLib art paths no longer use fixed path/name buffers
evidence: idToNameFun() now builds std::string paths with id/name validation, and image-counting uses string card names instead of fixed char tmp[254] / char cardname[200] buffers.
why it matters: Long names or corrupted data are less likely to overwrite stack memory, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a long-name art harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static inspection; CardArtLib make dry-runs; long-name art copy after build support exists.

title: Drawcardlib frame-name formatting now uses bounded diagnostics
evidence: get_pichandlename() now uses larger static diagnostic buffers plus snprintf instead of sprintf into char buf[3][160].
why it matters: Long base paths or config names are less likely to overwrite memory, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a long install path/config-name harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static grep for removed fixed diagnostic buffer/sprintf patterns; drawcardlib make dry-runs; long-path config copy after build support exists.

title: Drawcardlib config key concatenation now avoids fixed 104-byte buffers
evidence: get_cfg_int2_raw() and cfg_font() now build allocated INI keys instead of strcpy/strcat into char buf[104].
why it matters: Long or malformed config keys are less likely to overwrite memory, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a long-key config harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static grep for removed strcpy/strcat key-building patterns; drawcardlib make dry-runs; long-key config copy after build support exists.

title: Draft custom-set parser now uses bounded line parsing
evidence: load_custom_set() now checks fopen results, uses bounded fgets line reads, strips line endings, validates set slots, caps custom-set cards at 1000, guards the 2000-entry rotisserie bitmap, and no %[ fscanf scans remain.
why it matters: Malformed DraftSets files are less likely to crash or corrupt memory, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a malformed DraftSets harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static grep for removed fscanf scans; source make dry-runs; malformed DraftSets copy after build support exists.

title: Draft mode output paths are still runtime-local, but output opens now fail closed
evidence: draft helpers still write draft_errors.txt, picks.txt, packs.txt, and deck files by relative path, but those output opens now use open_draft_output(); invalid AI picks are skipped before score-log dereference.
why it matters: Wrong working directory can still dirty runtime roots, but missing or unwritable output files are less likely to crash draft flow.
suggested fix: Keep CWD behavior documented; consider a configurable output directory only after a draft-mode copy test.
risk of fix: safe for guard; moderate for relocation
test strategy: Static grep, exact-match parity checker, and future manual draft-mode test in disposable copy.

title: Program/Shandalar.exe adjacent runtime layout gaps are mitigated
evidence: Program/zlib.dll now matches root zlib.dll at 9f8729ac49e0ccea86fe3b1a9b2c3fae9986ecd09db92853e7a588dbda85bf90 in both this checkout and the local MTG copied install; bounded exact-path log loaded Program zlib/image/DrawCardLib/DECKDLL without the old fatal loader pattern; later drawcardlib.dll findings for missing Program\CARDART\ManaSymbols.pic and Program\CARDART\modern\Triggering.png are addressed by Program CardArt asset copies.
why it matters: Other copied installs can still drift, and users can still launch the wrong layout before the copied Program path is visibly tested after the Triggering.png copy.
suggested fix: Verify Program\zlib.dll and Program CardArt assets in any other copied install; keep root Shandalar as primary until exact Program path passes visibly.
test strategy: CrossOver/native visible launch from C:\Shandalar\Program; verify zlib and Program CardArt hashes first.

title: Root and Program Magic.exe differ but are both launch-relevant
evidence: Docs and hashes show different SHA-256 values; root Shandalar opens root Magic.exe while launcher uses Program/Magic.exe.
why it matters: Patching/testing only one copy leaves behavior inconsistent.
suggested fix: Always document exact path/hash and keep patch verification for both copies.
test strategy: Separate launch and hash verification for both Magic.exe paths.

title: Root and Program ManalinkEh.dll differ by hash and patch offset
evidence: Damage-prevention and AI clamp patch sites differ by path: root uses 0x3bb035, 0x44cb23/0x495a30, and 0x40d0e1/0x495a60; Program uses 0x381a25, 0x40f115/0x452c30, and 0x3d2da1/0x452c60.
why it matters: Copying the wrong DLL beside a Magic.exe path can miss or mismatch runtime fixes.
suggested fix: Use path-specific hash/offset verification before copying to any install.
test strategy: xxd/shasum both adjacent DLLs and copied install DLLs.

title: Declared-attacker undo may not reverse attack costs or attack-choice triggers
evidence: Patch clears STATE_ATTACKING before Done; docs note attack costs/triggers can run before/around selection.
why it matters: Undo may be safe for ordinary attackers but wrong for unusual costs/triggers.
suggested fix: Keep patch documented as conservative and require manual cases for costs, triggers, vigilance, and banding.
test strategy: Manual gameplay checklist R3/R4 plus edge-case attacker tests.

title: Damage-prevention prompts may still freeze without manual proof
evidence: Source snapshots and runtime DLLs now guard generic activated GAA_DAMAGE_PREVENTION* paths in granted_generic_activated_ability(); generated inventory records 127 rows covered by that helper and 2 Samite/Femeref/Kithkin rows with same-function guards.
why it matters: The runtime guard is hash/offset verified in root, Program, and the local MTG copied install, but no visible gameplay repro has proven that all prompt freezes are gone.
suggested fix: Run manual Femeref/Samite/Kithkin and generic damage-prevention repros, plus ordinary spell-casting turns, before calling the freeze class fixed.
test strategy: Runtime hash/offset proof for both ManalinkEh.dll paths plus manual repro tests by card.

title: Unimplemented DDBM/subtype flags assert instead of graceful fallback
evidence: Regex/TODO search finds ASSERT lines for unimplemented damage_dealt_* and subtype_deals_damage modes.
why it matters: If a new card path reaches these flags, the game may abort instead of failing gracefully.
suggested fix: Keep in backlog; convert to recoverable behavior only with card regression tests.
test strategy: Targeted scenario tests for each flag before any source/runtime change.

title: GDI object/DC lifetime is hand-managed in many UI paths
evidence: GDI search finds repeated CreateCompatibleDC/Bitmap, SelectObject, DeleteObject, DeleteDC, ReleaseDC patterns.
why it matters: Leaks or invalid selected-object deletion can destabilize long sessions.
suggested fix: Audit functions one at a time and add restore/delete conventions in source.
test strategy: Static review plus long-session GDI handle monitoring on Windows.

title: Manalink launcher mutates local Program/Mods trees
evidence: Launcher deletes temp folders, renames sound/stat folders, installs mods, and deletes draft decks relative to %~dp0.
why it matters: It is useful but can change runtime assets; package-root choice matters.
suggested fix: Run only in a test copy for cleanup/install-root investigations.
test strategy: Disposable-copy launch/menu test; compare file tree before/after.

title: Optional static analysis coverage is missing
evidence: cppcheck, scan-build, clang-tidy, infer, and semgrep are not installed.
why it matters: Current audit is regex/static reading, not semantic analysis.
suggested fix: Add optional analyzer docs or CI after build prerequisites are settled.
test strategy: Re-run tools when installed; store reports under docs/generated/code-audit.

title: Auto-target text parser now uses bounded line parsing
evidence: TargetsHuman.txt and TargetsAI.txt loading now checks fopen results, uses bounded fgets line reads, parses numeric dot-prefixed lines without %[ fscanf scans, and reserves one auto_targets slot for the -1 sentinel.
why it matters: Malformed or missing target-list files are less likely to crash or corrupt memory, though runtime behavior is not proven without a build/test environment.
suggested fix: Add a malformed TargetsHuman.txt/TargetsAI.txt harness after build support exists.
test strategy: Static grep for removed fscanf scans; source make dry-runs; malformed target-list copy after build support exists.
